_Fashionable wayfinders_ allow you to be dressed for success in almost any situation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _fashionable wayfinder_ can attune to up to three sets of mundane clothing in your possession during your daily preparations. A _fashionable wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You call one of the attuned sets of clothing onto yourself, changing clothes instantly and leaving you either holding the old set of clothing or with the old set folded neatly on the floor if you don't have a free hand to hold it. This activation can't change you into or out of a suit of armor of any kind. While the activation can transport the chosen attuned set of clothing onto you from up to 60 feet away, it can't transport any other creatures or items that might have been stored in the clothing.

_Homeward wayfinders_ are powerful escape tools in the hands of a Pathfinder agent, drawing upon a special connection to the Maze of the Open Road. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _homeward wayfinder_ attunes itself to the most recent Pathfinder Lodge you've visited and has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You teleport back to the attuned Pathfinder Lodge, as long as you are no more than 100 miles away. This [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect fails if you would bring another creature with you, even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space.

_Hummingbird wayfinders_ blend a few Arcadian secrets into the original _wayfinder_ design, allowing the _wayfinder_ to gain mobility for a time and act as a spy. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _hummingbird wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cause the _wayfinder_ to transform into a Tiny bee hummingbird, only 2 inches long. Other than the _wayfinder_ being a visible bird, this has the effects of [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239). In addition to [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) through the hummingbird, you can activate any [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) within the _wayfinder_ as if the _wayfinder_ were still on your person. When the effect ends, the _wayfinder_ transforms back to normal in its current position. Damaging the bird ends the effect but does not destroy the _wayfinder_.

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinders have access._

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. A _wayfinder_ functions as a compass.

 An indentation in the middle of the _wayfinder_ can hold a single _aeon stone_. Placing an _aeon stone_ in this indentation provides you all the benefits of having the _aeon stone_ orbiting your head, but it protects the stone from being noticed or stolen as easily. You invest a _wayfinder_ and the _aeon stone_ within it simultaneously, and they count as only one item toward your investiture limit. An invested _aeon stone_ slotted in a _wayfinder_ also grants its resonant power.

 If you have more than one _wayfinder_ with an invested _aeon stone_ on your person at a given time, destructive interference from their resonance prevents you from gaining benefits from any of them. You can still benefit from additional _aeon stones_ orbiting your head, just not in _wayfinders_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The _wayfinder_ is targeted by a 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Archaic Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 17

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to the archaic wayfinder_

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

</column>

---

An _archaic wayfinder_ functions as a compass and acts as a repository for one cantrip cast into it. To fill an _archaic wayfinder_, a spellcaster casts the chosen cantrip into the empty device, providing any necessary cost; the spell cannot be one that is normally cast as a free action or reaction. Activating an _archaic wayfinder_ is the same as the casting for the relevant spell. Once it has been activated, it becomes empty again. An _archaic wayfinder_ can hold an aeon stone and activate its resonant power. An _archaic wayfinder_ counts against the limit of one _wayfinder_ with a slotted resonant _[aeon stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)_.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Wayfinder of Rescue
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave](/Sources.aspx?ID=9) pg. 42

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

As a standard _wayfinder_, but once per day you can activate it to cast _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ on a creature other than yourself, using either the one-action or two-action version of _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ as a 1st-level spell.

level="2"

Bi-Resonant Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

---

A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ is inlaid with intricate platinum wires in a complex array of conduits for magical energy. These conduits control the resonant interference of _aeon stones_. A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ works just like a regular _wayfinder_ except it has two _aeon stone_ slots, and the _aeon stones_ in both slots grant their resonant powers without interfering with each other. The _bi-resonant wayfinder_ and the two _aeon stones_ count as two items toward your investiture limit.

A _shining wayfinder_ is a polished silver compass blessed by the power of benevolent deities. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_, except instead of an activation to cast _light_, the shining wayfinder has the following two activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You hold the _wayfinder_ and ask for guidance. The wayfinder casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the wayfinder’s divine blessings for yourself and your allies. The wayfinder casts [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), with an emanation centered on itself. Once per turn, you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to increase the emanation’s radius by 5 feet.

level="2"

pfs="Standard"

Vanishing Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

A _vanishing wayfinder_ is crafted of ivory chased with gold and silver. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_ and the following additional activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You turn the _wayfinder_ in a circle while invoking its command word, gaining the effects of a 2nd-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell for 5 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"

Chronicler Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Players can gain access to faction-specific gear by taking the corresponding Faction Gear Access Game Reward, available when they reach 20 reputation with the respective faction._

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

---

_Chronicler wayfinders_ bear a mystical connection to the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ themselves, granting them the ability to assist in situations where unusual lore is needed by drawing on lore from a particular topic. Each _chronicler wayfinder_ is attuned to a specific [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill, chosen at the time of creation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a chronicler wayfinder has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You tap into the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ on the _chronicler wayfinder's_ attuned topic, gaining the effects of a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action with the chosen Lore skill. For the Lore check's modifier, use whichever is better of +12 or your own modifier, using your level as your proficiency bonus even if you're untrained in the attuned Lore skill.

level="2"

In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, these _wayfinders_ each grant the ability to cast a particular elemental spell once per day, and can assist Pathfinders in exploring areas associated with their element. Additionally, you dig twice as quickly as normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _wayfinder_ casts a 4th-level [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312).

Additionally, you gain the constant effects of [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=370).

_Fashionable wayfinders_ allow you to be dressed for success in almost any situation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _fashionable wayfinder_ can attune to up to three sets of mundane clothing in your possession during your daily preparations. A _fashionable wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You call one of the attuned sets of clothing onto yourself, changing clothes instantly and leaving you either holding the old set of clothing or with the old set folded neatly on the floor if you don't have a free hand to hold it. This activation can't change you into or out of a suit of armor of any kind. While the activation can transport the chosen attuned set of clothing onto you from up to 60 feet away, it can't transport any other creatures or items that might have been stored in the clothing.

_Homeward wayfinders_ are powerful escape tools in the hands of a Pathfinder agent, drawing upon a special connection to the Maze of the Open Road. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _homeward wayfinder_ attunes itself to the most recent Pathfinder Lodge you've visited and has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You teleport back to the attuned Pathfinder Lodge, as long as you are no more than 100 miles away. This [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect fails if you would bring another creature with you, even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hummingbird Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Hummingbird wayfinders_ blend a few Arcadian secrets into the original _wayfinder_ design, allowing the _wayfinder_ to gain mobility for a time and act as a spy. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _hummingbird wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cause the _wayfinder_ to transform into a Tiny bee hummingbird, only 2 inches long. Other than the _wayfinder_ being a visible bird, this has the effects of [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239). In addition to [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) through the hummingbird, you can activate any [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) within the _wayfinder_ as if the _wayfinder_ were still on your person. When the effect ends, the _wayfinder_ transforms back to normal in its current position. Damaging the bird ends the effect but does not destroy the _wayfinder_.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinders have access._

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. A _wayfinder_ functions as a compass.

 An indentation in the middle of the _wayfinder_ can hold a single _aeon stone_. Placing an _aeon stone_ in this indentation provides you all the benefits of having the _aeon stone_ orbiting your head, but it protects the stone from being noticed or stolen as easily. You invest a _wayfinder_ and the _aeon stone_ within it simultaneously, and they count as only one item toward your investiture limit. An invested _aeon stone_ slotted in a _wayfinder_ also grants its resonant power.

 If you have more than one _wayfinder_ with an invested _aeon stone_ on your person at a given time, destructive interference from their resonance prevents you from gaining benefits from any of them. You can still benefit from additional _aeon stones_ orbiting your head, just not in _wayfinders_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The _wayfinder_ is targeted by a 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Archaic Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 17

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to the archaic wayfinder_

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

</column>

---

An _archaic wayfinder_ functions as a compass and acts as a repository for one cantrip cast into it. To fill an _archaic wayfinder_, a spellcaster casts the chosen cantrip into the empty device, providing any necessary cost; the spell cannot be one that is normally cast as a free action or reaction. Activating an _archaic wayfinder_ is the same as the casting for the relevant spell. Once it has been activated, it becomes empty again. An _archaic wayfinder_ can hold an aeon stone and activate its resonant power. An _archaic wayfinder_ counts against the limit of one _wayfinder_ with a slotted resonant _[aeon stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)_.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Wayfinder of Rescue
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave](/Sources.aspx?ID=9) pg. 42

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

As a standard _wayfinder_, but once per day you can activate it to cast _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ on a creature other than yourself, using either the one-action or two-action version of _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ as a 1st-level spell.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bi-Resonant Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 110

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ is inlaid with intricate platinum wires in a complex array of conduits for magical energy. These conduits control the resonant interference of _aeon stones_. A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ works just like a regular _wayfinder_ except it has two _aeon stone_ slots, and the _aeon stones_ in both slots grant their resonant powers without interfering with each other. The _bi-resonant wayfinder_ and the two _aeon stones_ count as two items toward your investiture limit.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shining Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

A _shining wayfinder_ is a polished silver compass blessed by the power of benevolent deities. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_, except instead of an activation to cast _light_, the shining wayfinder has the following two activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You hold the _wayfinder_ and ask for guidance. The wayfinder casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the wayfinder’s divine blessings for yourself and your allies. The wayfinder casts [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), with an emanation centered on itself. Once per turn, you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to increase the emanation’s radius by 5 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Vanishing Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

A _vanishing wayfinder_ is crafted of ivory chased with gold and silver. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_ and the following additional activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You turn the _wayfinder_ in a circle while invoking its command word, gaining the effects of a 2nd-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell for 5 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"

Chronicler Wayfinder

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 30

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Players can gain access to faction-specific gear by taking the corresponding Faction Gear Access Game Reward, available when they reach 20 reputation with the respective faction._

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Chronicler wayfinders_ bear a mystical connection to the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ themselves, granting them the ability to assist in situations where unusual lore is needed by drawing on lore from a particular topic. Each _chronicler wayfinder_ is attuned to a specific [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill, chosen at the time of creation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a chronicler wayfinder has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You tap into the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ on the _chronicler wayfinder's_ attuned topic, gaining the effects of a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action with the chosen Lore skill. For the Lore check's modifier, use whichever is better of +12 or your own modifier, using your level as your proficiency bonus even if you're untrained in the attuned Lore skill.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Razmiri Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 72

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

Worn on a chain around the neck, a _Razmiri wayfinder_ functions as a [compass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=16) and provides its wearer a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) or [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46). _Razmiri wayfinders_ can also be used to grant temporary healing, making it easier for the wearer to impersonate a divine spellcaster.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You touch an adjacent creature, granting them 3d8 temporary Hit Points for 1 hour.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> manipulate;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch an adjacent creature and attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against one [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) afflicting the creature. On a success, you halt the effects of the poison or disease for 1 day, rather than removing it. This temporarily relieves any penalties imposed by the affliction but doesn't cure the effect or count against its duration; the poison or disease is merely halted for a time, and it resumes where it left off once this effect ends.

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Fashionable wayfinders_ allow you to be dressed for success in almost any situation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _fashionable wayfinder_ can attune to up to three sets of mundane clothing in your possession during your daily preparations. A _fashionable wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You call one of the attuned sets of clothing onto yourself, changing clothes instantly and leaving you either holding the old set of clothing or with the old set folded neatly on the floor if you don't have a free hand to hold it. This activation can't change you into or out of a suit of armor of any kind. While the activation can transport the chosen attuned set of clothing onto you from up to 60 feet away, it can't transport any other creatures or items that might have been stored in the clothing.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Homeward Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Homeward wayfinders_ are powerful escape tools in the hands of a Pathfinder agent, drawing upon a special connection to the Maze of the Open Road. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _homeward wayfinder_ attunes itself to the most recent Pathfinder Lodge you've visited and has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You teleport back to the attuned Pathfinder Lodge, as long as you are no more than 100 miles away. This [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect fails if you would bring another creature with you, even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hummingbird Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Hummingbird wayfinders_ blend a few Arcadian secrets into the original _wayfinder_ design, allowing the _wayfinder_ to gain mobility for a time and act as a spy. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _hummingbird wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cause the _wayfinder_ to transform into a Tiny bee hummingbird, only 2 inches long. Other than the _wayfinder_ being a visible bird, this has the effects of [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239). In addition to [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) through the hummingbird, you can activate any [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) within the _wayfinder_ as if the _wayfinder_ were still on your person. When the effect ends, the _wayfinder_ transforms back to normal in its current position. Damaging the bird ends the effect but does not destroy the _wayfinder_.
name: Homeward Wayfinder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinders have access._

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

This compact compass repurposes ancient technology to draw fantastic powers from the mysterious magical items called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407). It serves as a badge of office for agents of the Pathfinder Society and as a status symbol among adventurers of any stripe. A _wayfinder_ functions as a compass.

 An indentation in the middle of the _wayfinder_ can hold a single _aeon stone_. Placing an _aeon stone_ in this indentation provides you all the benefits of having the _aeon stone_ orbiting your head, but it protects the stone from being noticed or stolen as easily. You invest a _wayfinder_ and the _aeon stone_ within it simultaneously, and they count as only one item toward your investiture limit. An invested _aeon stone_ slotted in a _wayfinder_ also grants its resonant power.

 If you have more than one _wayfinder_ with an invested _aeon stone_ on your person at a given time, destructive interference from their resonance prevents you from gaining benefits from any of them. You can still benefit from additional _aeon stones_ orbiting your head, just not in _wayfinders_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The _wayfinder_ is targeted by a 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Archaic Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 17

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to the archaic wayfinder_

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

</column>

---

An _archaic wayfinder_ functions as a compass and acts as a repository for one cantrip cast into it. To fill an _archaic wayfinder_, a spellcaster casts the chosen cantrip into the empty device, providing any necessary cost; the spell cannot be one that is normally cast as a free action or reaction. Activating an _archaic wayfinder_ is the same as the casting for the relevant spell. Once it has been activated, it becomes empty again. An _archaic wayfinder_ can hold an aeon stone and activate its resonant power. An _archaic wayfinder_ counts against the limit of one _wayfinder_ with a slotted resonant _[aeon stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407)_.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Wayfinder of Rescue
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Scenario #1-03: Escaping the Grave](/Sources.aspx?ID=9) pg. 42

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

As a standard _wayfinder_, but once per day you can activate it to cast _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ on a creature other than yourself, using either the one-action or two-action version of _[heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)_ as a 1st-level spell.

<title

pfs="Standard"

Bi-Resonant Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 110

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ is inlaid with intricate platinum wires in a complex array of conduits for magical energy. These conduits control the resonant interference of _aeon stones_. A _bi-resonant wayfinder_ works just like a regular _wayfinder_ except it has two _aeon stone_ slots, and the _aeon stones_ in both slots grant their resonant powers without interfering with each other. The _bi-resonant wayfinder_ and the two _aeon stones_ count as two items toward your investiture limit.

<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

A _shining wayfinder_ is a polished silver compass blessed by the power of benevolent deities. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_, except instead of an activation to cast _light_, the shining wayfinder has the following two activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You hold the _wayfinder_ and ask for guidance. The wayfinder casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the wayfinder’s divine blessings for yourself and your allies. The wayfinder casts [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), with an emanation centered on itself. Once per turn, you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to increase the emanation’s radius by 5 feet.

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

A _vanishing wayfinder_ is crafted of ivory chased with gold and silver. It has the usual functions of a _wayfinder_ and the following additional activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You turn the _wayfinder_ in a circle while invoking its command word, gaining the effects of a 2nd-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell for 5 minutes.

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Players can gain access to faction-specific gear by taking the corresponding Faction Gear Access Game Reward, available when they reach 20 reputation with the respective faction._

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

_Chronicler wayfinders_ bear a mystical connection to the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ themselves, granting them the ability to assist in situations where unusual lore is needed by drawing on lore from a particular topic. Each _chronicler wayfinder_ is attuned to a specific [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill, chosen at the time of creation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a chronicler wayfinder has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You tap into the _Pathfinder Chronicles_ on the _chronicler wayfinder's_ attuned topic, gaining the effects of a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action with the chosen Lore skill. For the Lore check's modifier, use whichever is better of +12 or your own modifier, using your level as your proficiency bonus even if you're untrained in the attuned Lore skill.

level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Razmiri Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 72

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

</column>

---

Worn on a chain around the neck, a _Razmiri wayfinder_ functions as a [compass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=16) and provides its wearer a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) or [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46). _Razmiri wayfinders_ can also be used to grant temporary healing, making it easier for the wearer to impersonate a divine spellcaster.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You touch an adjacent creature, granting them 3d8 temporary Hit Points for 1 hour.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> manipulate;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch an adjacent creature and attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against one [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) afflicting the creature. On a success, you halt the effects of the poison or disease for 1 day, rather than removing it. This temporarily relieves any penalties imposed by the affliction but doesn't cure the effect or count against its duration; the poison or disease is merely halted for a time, and it resumes where it left off once this effect ends.

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

_Hummingbird wayfinders_ blend a few Arcadian secrets into the original _wayfinder_ design, allowing the _wayfinder_ to gain mobility for a time and act as a spy. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _hummingbird wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cause the _wayfinder_ to transform into a Tiny bee hummingbird, only 2 inches long. Other than the _wayfinder_ being a visible bird, this has the effects of [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239). In addition to [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) through the hummingbird, you can activate any [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) within the _wayfinder_ as if the _wayfinder_ were still on your person. When the effect ends, the _wayfinder_ transforms back to normal in its current position. Damaging the bird ends the effect but does not destroy the _wayfinder_.
name: Hummingbird Wayfinder

---

This delicate necklace contains bottled whispers distilled from a source on the Astral Plane rumored to be connected to the first lie ever told. While wearing the necklace, you gain a +3 item bonus to Deception checks, and you can attempt to counteract effects that would force you to tell the truth or determine whether you are lying. Success on this counteract attempt lets you ignore the effect, rather than removing the effect entirely. The counteract level is 9, with a counteract modifier of +35.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact, envision, command;     **Effect** You unstopper the vial and release the lie, creating the effect of a [_fabricated truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=106) (DC 47). The vial is emptied and can never be activated again.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _fabricated truth_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whisper of the First Lie

When created, this mutagen is attuned to your choice of one of four energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. When consumed, the mutagen suffuses your body with energy that spills out of you whenever you attack. At higher levels, it can even grant you the ability to unleash the energy in controlled bursts.

**Benefit** You gain resistance to the attuned energy type. Whenever you score a hit with a melee weapon, add the listed amount of damage of the attuned energy type.

**Drawback** You gain weakness 5 to the other three energy types.

**Trigger** Your attack roll with the _Cinderclaw gauntlet_ is a critical success.

 
</row>

---

This sharp-taloned _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11) _appears to have been crafted from dragon scales, with claws built from iron. In addition to the typical weapon traits, a _Cinderclaw gauntlet_ has the [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait.

 On a critical hit, the _Cinderclaw gauntlet_ deals an additional 1d6 fire damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>command</row></row>

**Frequency** once per day

**Trigger** You would miss with an attack made with a bow.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This arrowhead-shaped charm is not meant to be affixed to an arrow, but instead to be carried in a pocket or inside of a quiver. A _hunter’s arrowhead_ is etched with images sacred to the elven god Ketephys. If you carry one on your person, you are infused with great skill at hunting, and gain a +1 item bonus to Survival skill checks and attack rolls against any creature you’ve currently designated as your prey for the [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10) ranger ability (note that the bonus to attack rolls is an item bonus, and therefore not cumulative with an item bonus from a magic weapon). A _hunter's arrowhead_ is also a religious symbol of Ketephys.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Arrowhead

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Heartstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=487)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 203

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gemstone grants its wearer a +2 item bonus to saving throws.

Each _heartstone_ is powered by the spirit of a specific night hag. If it’s separated from her for 24 hours (or she’s been dead for 24 hours), it becomes a nonmagical gemstone. A _heartstone_ allows the hag to use additional occult innate spells: _[ethereal jaunt](/Spells.aspx?ID=105)_ (9th level) and _[bind soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=21)_ at will, and _[shadow blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273)_ twice per day.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;    **Requirements** You must be touching the _heartstone_.; **Effect** The _heartstone_ attempts to counteract one disease affecting you (counteract level 5, counteract modifier +18).

**Craft Requirements** You must be a night hag.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Heartstone
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 203

**Price** 600 gp ((200 gp when nonmagical))

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs=""
>
Skarja's Heartstone
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 89

**Price** 2,500 gp ((1,000 gp when nonmagical))

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This pale gemstone grants its wearer a +2 item bonus to saving throws and the ability to discern a target's alignment (as [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65), but can detect chaotic, evil, good, and lawful auras simultaneously) at will by peering through the gemstone.

 Like all hag _heartstones_, this gemstone is powered by [Skarja's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2110) spirit. Skarja does not need to wear her _heartstone_ to benefit from its powers, but she cannot use them if she hasn't touched her _heartstone_ in the past 7 days. If Skarja's _heartstone_ is separated from her for more than 7 days (or if she's been dead for 7 days), it becomes a non-magical gemstone. Skarja's _heartstone_ allows her to use additional occult innate spells: [_ethereal jaunt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=105) (9th level) and [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21) at will, [_shadow blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) (6th level) twice per day, and [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=301) once per day.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;    **Requirements** You must be touching the _heartstone_; **Effect** The _heartstone_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) affecting you (counteract level 7, counteract modifier +23).
name: Heartstone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Lich Soul Cage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=488)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 222

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item is crafted by a spellcaster who wishes to become a lich. When a lich is destroyed, its soul flees to the soul cage. The soul cage then rebuilds the lich's undead body over the course of 1d10 days. Afterward, the lich manifests next to the soul cage, fully healed and in a new body (therefore, it lacks any equipment it had on its old body). A lich's soul cage must be destroyed to prevent a lich from returning.

The standard soul cage is a sealed metal box containing strips of parchment inscribed with magical phrases. This box has Hardness 9 and 36 HP, but some liches devise more durable or difficult-to-obtain soul cages. A soul cage might also come in the form of a ring, an amulet, or a similar item; the specifics are up to the creator.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lich Soul Cage

---

This glowing gem is harvested from a demilich and has an 8th-level spell magically bonded to it. This item has the traits of the spell it contains.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The gem casts the 8th-level spell it contains. This activation uses the same number of actions as Casting the Spell. Once the spell is cast, the gem's glow fades, but returns 24 hours later, when the spell can be used once again.

**Craft Requirements** _Demilich eye gems_ can't be crafted.

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary](/Sources.aspx?ID=2) pg. 313

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potent weapon used by [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=394) is a _+4 [greater corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) obsidian [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)_ that grants a +4 item bonus to attack rolls, deals an extra 2d6 damage to plants, and has the properties of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141))   **Requirements** You aren't wielding _Blackaxe_ and you are its true owner. ; **Effect** You sense the world around _Blackaxe_ as though you were in its location and can use any of your innate spells through the link as if it were the source of the spell. If another creature is wielding _Blackaxe_, it must succeed at a DC 50 Will save or be [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until it relinquishes the weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156))   **Requirements** You aren't wielding _Blackaxe_ and you are its true owner.; **Effect** _Blackaxe_ appears in your hands, teleporting instantly from its prior location.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Make a Strike against a living tree with _Blackaxe_. If it hits, the tree withers to ash and you heal 250 Hit Points and gain the benefit of [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (6th) and [_restore senses_](/Spells.aspx?ID=259).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blackaxe

---

These bioluminescent jellyfish are as bright as candles, come in a variety of colors, and can act as living lanterns. They dry up and die if removed from the water for more than 1 hour, but as long as they’re allowed access to seawater, they can usually survive for up to 1 year by feeding on micronutrients. Rarely, a well-treated jellyfish lamp might live longer and potentially develop stronger light or additional abilities.

</column>

>
[Sun Orchid Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=497)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you drink this elixir, you physically become as you were in whatever phase of your youth you desire. Your new body begins in peak health, regardless of what your actual condition was at that age. Any physical or mental imperfections— including scars, lost limbs, and curses, diseases, poisons of 20th level or lower— are removed, and you regain your full Hit Points. You immediately begin to age normally again, with your natural lifespan extended as if you had never aged past your new apparent age. You retain all your memories.

 Unlike Crafting most items, brewing the sun orchid elixir takes 1 month’s time. While an individual vial of sun orchid elixir isn’t unique, the formula is unique, and it’s known only to Artokus Kirran of Thuvia.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Orchid Elixir

[Final Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=500)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=40" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 126, [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 53

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 40
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A Large or smaller restrained or willing creature can be executed by a _final blade_. As an artifact, a _final blade_ cannot be harmed by any but a very specific means.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);     **Effect** You execute a creature restrained beneath the blade. The executed creature takes 11d10 slashing damage and must succeed at a DC 45 Fortitude save or be decapitated as though it suffered a critical hit with a natural 20 from a _+3 major striking [vorpal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39)_. The soul of a creature executed with a _final blade_ is trapped, and the creature can’t be returned to life through any means, even a [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or similar magic. A _final blade_ can hold any number of souls in this way, and they can be released only through a complex ritual known only to the Gray Gardeners or through the destruction of the _final blade_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Final Blade

---

This charm, a dragon-shaped pendant worn like a necklace or set into armor or clothing as a decoration, is warm and smooth to the touch. It must be held in one hand to be used (pressing the palm of one’s hand to the charm also works, provided that your hand is otherwise empty). It has four distinct powers.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _dragon’s eye charm_ manifests fire for you to wield against your foe. You cast a 5th-level [_produce flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) spell with a spell attack roll of +13.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The charm’s pupil becomes plainly visible. You can look through the charm to gain [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) for as long as you Sustain the Activation, to a maximum of 10 minutes.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _dragon’s eye charm_ shrouds you in a faintly glowing layer of golden energy. You gain fire resistance 5 for 10 minutes.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You can speak and understand [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) as long as you Sustain the Activation, to a maximum of 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon's Eye Charm

---

This _+1 [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) striking [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=44)_ has an ornate central grip that depicts a silver dragon, the neck, wings, and tail of which wrap over and around the handle in knots to support the weapon’s four cold iron blades. While the handle is polished to a mirrorlike shine, the blades of _Eclipse_ are a flat black and entirely unreflective. _Eclipse_ is also the portal key required to activate Dreamgate at Alseta’s Ring.

 Whenever you score a critical hit with _Eclipse_, the target creature’s eyes are plunged into darkness; if that creature doesn’t have [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), it is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** _Eclipse’s_ blades glow like starlit night sky, causing it to shed light as a torch until you deactivate it as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** three times per day; **Trigger** You attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), but you haven’t rolled yet;  **Effect** You absorb light, gaining a +2 status bonus to the Stealth check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eclipse

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

---

Certain Scarlet Triad poisoners use toxins like addlebrain to keep captured prisoners docile and compliant for short periods of time until they can be properly shackled and imprisoned. Addlebrain is distilled from a hallucinogenic lichen that can often be found growing in the sewers below particularly large cities like Katapesh.

 Once soaked in special reagents and then dried, a dose of addlebrain must be either lit with flame or tossed into a fire to activate, whereupon it unleashes a cloud of toxin as normal for an inhaled poison.

 **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 1 day; **Stage 1** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (10 minutes); **Stage 2** enfeebled 2 and stupefied 2 (1 hour); **Stage 3** [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), enfeebled 4, and stupefied 4 (1 day)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Addlebrain

</traits>

**Bulk** 1

<row gap="medium">

---

Different versions of this belt are customized to appear to suit specific trades, so a belt used for carpentry would look somewhat different than a belt for baking.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** You place an object of up to 1 Bulk into the belt, transforming that object into a tool befitting the trade for which the belt was created. As long as there are enough transformed items, the belt can be used as artisan’s tools for that trade.

 Each object remains transformed until it has been removed from the belt for 24 hours or someone uses a single Interact action to return it to its normal form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bellflower Toolbelt

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

</row>

---

This mid-sized, silver cauldron is a boon within areas where access to fresh food is limited, for it can be commanded to fill itself with hearty (and delicious) vegetable stew. It can also be put to a much greater use in the pursuit of crafting certain items. When used to Craft alchemical items, potions, or oils, a bountiful cauldron grants a +2 item bonus to the Crafting check.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You stir the cauldron, and it casts a 4th-level [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=52) spell, filling itself with enough delicious vegetable stew to feed 12 Medium creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bountiful Cauldron

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes **Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A _dreamstone_ is a disc-shaped object carved with imagery or words sacred to [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5)—be it her religious symbol, a short prayer, or merely the shape of a single star. When you carry a _dreamstone_, you find it easier to fall asleep, and you gain improved effects from the resulting rest. You always fall asleep within 5 minutes of lying down with the intention of sleeping, and you require only 2 hours of sleep per day to gain the benefits of 8 hours of sleep, provided you have carried the _dreamstone_ for at least 24 hours prior to resting. As long as you carry a _dreamstone_, you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against sleep effects. **Activate** Interact (metamagic); **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell of 4th level or lower that has the [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) trait or is associated with dreams, the spell slot is not expended. Dreamstone **Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A _dreamstone_ is a disc-shaped object carved with imagery or words sacred to [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5)—be it her religious symbol, a short prayer, or merely the shape of a single star. When you carry a _dreamstone_, you find it easier to fall asleep, and you gain improved effects from the resulting rest. You always fall asleep within 5 minutes of lying down with the intention of sleeping, and you require only 2 hours of sleep per day to gain the benefits of 8 hours of sleep, provided you have carried the _dreamstone_ for at least 24 hours prior to resting. As long as you carry a _dreamstone_, you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against sleep effects. **Activate** Interact (metamagic); **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell of 4th level or lower that has the [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) trait or is associated with dreams, the spell slot is not expended. Cursed Dreamstone **Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A _dreamstone_ can become cursed if left exposed to creatures that corrupt sleep, generate nightmares, or otherwise prey on sleeping or dreaming victims via supernatural methods. A _cursed dreamstone_ seems to function as a normal _dreamstone_ until the bearer falls asleep or is forced to attempt a saving throw against a sleep effect. At this point, the person carrying the cursed _dreamstone_ must attempt a DC 26 Will save to resist the curse’s effects. **Critical Success** The character resists the curse entirely and experiences a vivid dream while sleeping that warns them of the nature of the _cursed dreamstone_.
**Success** The character resists the curse.
**Failure** As long as the cursed character possesses the stone, they are fatigued whenever they wake from sleep, whether natural or magically induced, and they take a –2 item penalty to all saving throws against sleep effects instead of gaining the normal bonus from a _dreamstone_.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and whenever the bearer attempts a saving throw against a sleep effect, they get the outcome that is one degree worse than the result of their saving throw.
name: Dreamstone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs=""
>
[Eye of the Wise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=508)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fist-sized, 12-rayed black star sapphire is sacred to [Yuelral](/Deities.aspx?ID=111), the elven goddess of magic and jewels, and most accounts of its creation attribute its crafting to a circle of her worshippers. In addition to the abilities listed below, the gem functions as a portal key for Jewelgate at Alseta's Ring.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You touch the _Eye of the Wise_ to a gem worth at least 10 gp, whereupon the _Eye of the Wise_ assumes the appearance of the touched gemstone. While this connection is active, the carriers of the _Eye of the Wise_ and of the other gemstone can communicate telepathically with one another over any distance, as long as both are on the same plane. This connection is broken if the other gemstone is damaged, if the _Eye of the Wise_ becomes attuned to another gemstone, or if the bearer of the _Eye of the Wise_ [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) the activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You hold the _Eye of the Wise_ up to your own eye and peer through it. The _Eye of the Wise_ grows transparent and grants you a +2 item bonus to attempts to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70), [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85). You can Sustain this activation as long as you hold the gem in place.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eye of the Wise

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Gourd Home](/Equipment.aspx?ID=509)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 72

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dried gourd seems entirely nondescript, as it is hollow and has a sturdy cord wrapped around its neck for easy carrying. Closer inspection reveals the gourd has grown in the rough shape of a hut or similar small dwelling.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) Interact;     **Effect** You place the _gourd home_ on the ground (in an open area large enough to contain it) and cause it to expand in size to a 30-foot-square structure with one door. The interior of the gourd is a single large room that can hold 10 Small or Medium creatures with moderate comfort.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The gourd home must be expanded into its house form atop earth or soil.; **Effect** By rapping on the door from either outside or inside, you cause the _gourd home_ to shrink back into its normal size and appear to be a non-magical gourd sitting on the ground. For the following 8 hours, the interior of the gourd home becomes an extradimensional space whose size appears unchanged to those within.

 If anyone outside picks up or damages the gourd, or if anyone inside the _gourd home_ attempts to exit, all creatures within the _gourd home_ immediately reappear in the area surrounding the gourd, and the _gourd home_ becomes inactive for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gourd Home

>
[Cloak of Repute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=510)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gaudy and extravagant cloak is adorned with brilliant gems and a fur trim. While wearing the cloak, you gain instant fame and notoriety, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to Make an Impression. Creatures attempting to learn about you gain the same bonus to Gather Information about you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wield your great stature to make a Request of a creature. If you roll a success on the check, you get a critical success instead.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Insistent Door Knocker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark iron door knocker comes in a variety of shapes, such as a bird clutching a ring in its talons or a gargoyle holding a ring in its mouth. Placing the door knocker on the surface of a door (an Interact action) causes it to attach and remain in place. While in place, the door knocker whispers hints to you while you attempt to unlock the door, granting a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to Pick a Lock. Removing the door knocker from a surface requires you to use an Interact action.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the door knocker against a wall, floor, or ceiling. The door knocker fuses in place and creates a usable door within the surface. Any creature can move through the door normally, as if the door had always been there. The door can penetrate up to 1 foot of material, so thick material, such as heavy stone walls, can cause this effect to fail, expending its use for the day. A thin layer of metal in a wall, floor, or ceiling also causes the effect to fail. You can use an Interact action to release the door knocker from the surface and return the surface to its previous shape.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Insistent Door Knocker
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Insistent Door Knocker (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the door knocker can penetrate up to 5 feet of material when activated.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Insistent Door Knocker (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

**Price** 13,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and the door knocker can penetrate up to 10 feet of material when activated.
name: Insistent Door Knocker

[Insistent Door Knocker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark iron door knocker comes in a variety of shapes, such as a bird clutching a ring in its talons or a gargoyle holding a ring in its mouth. Placing the door knocker on the surface of a door (an Interact action) causes it to attach and remain in place. While in place, the door knocker whispers hints to you while you attempt to unlock the door, granting a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to Pick a Lock. Removing the door knocker from a surface requires you to use an Interact action.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the door knocker against a wall, floor, or ceiling. The door knocker fuses in place and creates a usable door within the surface. Any creature can move through the door normally, as if the door had always been there. The door can penetrate up to 1 foot of material, so thick material, such as heavy stone walls, can cause this effect to fail, expending its use for the day. A thin layer of metal in a wall, floor, or ceiling also causes the effect to fail. You can use an Interact action to release the door knocker from the surface and return the surface to its previous shape.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This small, crystalline carving takes the shape of a tree leaf and attaches to armor or clothing with a simple strap. When you activate the _snapleaf_, you gain the benefits of [_feather fall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) and a 2nd-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell for 1 minute or until you stop falling, whichever comes first.

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** 1 --- This long, shoddy cloak bears a hood and a simple brass clasp. While wearing the cloak, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to Impersonate an individual and to Lie while in character as that individual. **Activate** 1 minute (envision, Interact); **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You are Impersonating someone else; **Effect** While conversing or otherwise casually interacting with other creatures, you can adjust the mantle’s clasp to modify those creatures’ recollections of the last 5 minutes of their interaction with you. Each creature must attempt a DC 25 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature recalls the interaction clearly and realizes you were trying to alter their memory.
**Success** The creature recalls the interaction clearly.
**Failure** You decide whether or not the creature recalls the interaction. You can also alter minor details the creature recalls about the interaction, such as the time, place, and whether the interaction was favorable, as determined by the GM.
**Critical Failure** You decide whether or not the creature recalls the interaction. You can also alter major details the creature recalls about the interaction, such as believing you to be another individual or that the creature agreed to a favor, as determined by the GM. Unmemorable Mantle **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 300 gp **Bulk** 1 Unmemorable Mantle (Greater) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 1,300 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus is +2, and the Will save DC is 28. Unmemorable Mantle (Major) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 21,750 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus is +3, the Will save DC is 38, and you can activate the mantle once every 10 minutes.
name: Unmemorable Mantle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armored Skirt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=514)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 91

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These armor-plated skirts, which are also known as armored kilts, are favored by Knights of Lastwall for their versatility and aesthetic appeal. An armored skirt can be donned with 2 Interact actions when it’s worn with light or medium armor, or as part of donning heavy armor.

 When worn with a [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47), [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43), [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46), or [scale mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=45), an armored skirt increases the armor’s item bonus to AC by 1, worsens the armor’s [check penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2173) by 1, reduces the armor’s Dex cap by 1, increases the [Strength](/Rules.aspx?ID=2175) score required to ignore the check penalty and reduce the [Speed penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2174) by 2, and adds the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=660) trait. This also makes the armor one step heavier (from light to medium, or medium to heavy), and you use the proficiency bonus appropriate to this adjusted armor type.

 Alternatively, when wearing an armored skirt to replace appropriate portions of a set of [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49) or [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50), reduce the armor’s item bonus to AC by 1, lessen the check penalty by 1, decrease the Strength score required to ignore the check penalty and reduce the Speed penalty by 2, increase the armor’s Dex cap by 1, and add the noisy trait to the armor. An armored skirt grants no benefit when worn by itself or with armors other than those listed here.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Armored Skirt

[Crimson Brand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=515)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 or 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Bastard Sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)
</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These ominous swords are decorated with crimson lacquer and serve as perfect conduits for the Crimson Oath’s power. The first was granted to Clarethe Iomedar by a mysterious patron who many Reclaimers believe to be an emissary of [Ragathiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=126). A _crimson brand_ is a _+1 striking [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)_. When the wielder uses the crimson brand to cast [_invoke the Crimson Oath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=556), they can generate a 60-foot line or a 30-foot cone instead of the normal 20-foot cone. Whenever the wielder would inflict [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on an undead creature with the _crimson brand_, they can instead wreathe the creature in ruby energy, inflicting the same amount of persistent positive damage instead of persistent bleed damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crimson Brand
