</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 92

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto heavy armor

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Sinister knight_ armor shrouds the wearer’s identity in secrecy, allowing Crimson Reclaimers to pass among foes without being immediately unmasked. The wearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** With a thought, the wearer activates a disguise. While the _sinister knight_ armor’s disguise is active, any identifying insignia or aesthetic of the armor is replaced by generic malevolent aesthetics such as spikes or demonic faces. While in the disguise, the wearer is always considered to be taking precautions against [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), and even a creature that successfully notices the wearer with its lifesense mistakes the wearer for an undead unless it critically succeeds at its Perception check or the wearer critically fails a Deception or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check. Finally, while in the disguise, the rune attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=299) any effects that would reveal your alignment; on a successful counteract check, rather than negate the effect, the rune causes the effect to perceive your alignment as evil (maintaining any lawful or chaotic component of your alignment).

 The wearer can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) the disguise, and if the wearer’s armor is removed, the disguise deactivates automatically.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sinister Knight

A _serrating_ weapon’s bladed edge separates into jagged, swirling shards that spin along the blade. When dealing slashing damage, the weapon deals an additional 1d4 damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You brandish the weapon and focus its power, causing the serrated shards to buzz as they spin at a dizzying speed. On your next hit with the weapon this turn that deals slashing damage, the _serrating_ rune adds an additional 1d12 damage instead of the additional 1d4 damage, and then the shards return to their usual speed.

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 93 **Price** 4,500 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 4 **Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- This elegant [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) is embossed with heraldic imagery and never tarnishes or loses its shine. _Glorious plate_ is _+2 resilient full plate_. In battle, _glorious plate_ sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius, which enemies see as a blinding halo that obscures creatures other than the wearer. When an enemy looks at the wearer, it must attempt a DC 33 Will saving throw. The enemy is then temporarily immune for 1 minute. **Success** The enemy is unaffected.
**Failure** For 1 minute, the wearer’s allies within the glorious plate’s 10-foot radius of light are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the enemy. Allies are concealed only while they remain within the light; if allies enter the light later, they are concealed as well.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the wearer’s allies are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to the enemy instead of concealed. **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The armor casts [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) on you.
name: Glorious Plate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scrollstaff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=521)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 100

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)
</row>

**Access** A character who is Magaambyan of at least attendant rank has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A band of pliant wood encircles the head of this slender mahogany staff. The Magaambya created the first _scrollstaves_ millennia ago, and even the few _scrollstaves_ found beyond the organization often incorporate Magaambyan iconography as an homage to their source.

 A _scrollstaff_ serves as a weapon just like an ordinary [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) and can be improved via runes like any other staff. In addition, if you are capable of creating magical scrolls, you can inscribe a single scroll onto the staff’s head for the same cost as normal for [Crafting](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) the scroll. If necessary, you can embed a required material focus into the staff as part of the same Craft activity. As long as you are holding the staff, you can cast the spell from the staff’s head. Casting the written spell works just like casting from a scroll, but you can perform the necessary material component with the staff, even if you don’t have a free hand. Once you cast the inscribed spell, any material focus remains embedded in the staff until removed as an Interact action.

 The staff can hold only a single scroll at a time. If you inscribe a new scroll before casting the previous spell, you replace the existing spell and remove any previous material focus as part of the Craft activity to write the new scroll.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scrollstaff

This coin usually resembles ancient currency. An intricate matrix of gold and platinum wires inside the coin causes it to float an inch in the air and spin if placed on a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;    **Requirements** The coin is levitating above a _wayfinder_; **Effect** You give the coin a message of 25 words or fewer. The message repeats in your voice the next time the coin is floated above a _wayfinder_. A _Pathfinder’s coin_ can hold only one message at a time, and it replays its message only once.

    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pathfinder's Pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=524)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 111

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt pouch

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This unobtrusive belt pouch is popular among Pathfinders who operate in places where the Society is not welcome. It is constantly under the effects of a 3rd-level [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) spell to appear non-magical. The pouch’s normal space holds as much as an ordinary belt pouch, but it also has a small extradimensional space that holds up to 1 Bulk of items. A creature actively inspecting the pouch in its ordinary state discovers the existence of the extradimensional space only on a critical success to Search the pouch (see the [Concealing an Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) action of the Stealth skill), though when the extradimensional space is active, it’s obvious to anyone examining the pouch that it is more than it seems.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You switch the pouch to allow access to its extradimensional space instead of its normal space, or vice versa. This lasts until you next activate it. Items in the other space are inaccessible and hard to find until you switch the pouch back.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pathfinder's Pouch

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 111

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The four different types of _scroll cases of simplicity_ often bear adornments appropriate to their magical tradition, such as angelic wings or otherworldly lettering. On the inside, intricate runic diagrams spiral out to surround the scroll stored within. A scroll placed within the case can be converted into energy to cast consistently useful spells depending on its type. You must be able to cast spells of a given tradition to use a _scroll case of simplicity_ of a corresponding type.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** The scroll case contains a single scroll of a 1st-level spell; **Effect** You transfer the scroll’s energy into the scroll case, consuming the scroll, and you can immediately begin casting one of the scroll case’s spells. If you use any action other than to Cast a Spell from the scroll case after activating the _scroll case of simplicity_, the scroll and its energy are lost.

<column gap="tiny">

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ancestral Echoing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=526)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Saggorak" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=269" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

**Price** 9,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The wisdom of this weapon’s past owners flows into your mind, amplifying your own abilities with the weapon. Your proficiency rank with this weapon is one step higher than normal, to a maximum of the highest proficiency rank you have in any weapon. For instance, if you had master proficieny with martial weapons and expert proficiency with advanced weapons, you would have master proficiency with advanced weapon that had this rune.

 In addition, while wielding the _ancestral echoing_ weapon, you have expert proficiency in one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill relevant to one or more of the weapon’s previous owners. This is typically Dwarven Lore but is ultimately determined by the GM depending on the weapon’s history.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ancestral Echoing

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Rock-Braced](/Equipment.aspx?ID=527)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Saggorak" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=269" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto medium or heavy armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Rock-braced_ armor makes you as hard to move as a boulder. Whenever another creature attempts to forcibly move you from your space, you gain a +4 item bonus to your Fortitude DC against the check to move you. If the creature would not normally need to attempt a check to move you, then the creature must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against your Fortitude DC (including the +4 item bonus) or you are unmoved.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rock-Braced

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Kin-Warding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=538)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a  clan dagger 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _kin-warding_ clan dagger can deflect attacks aimed at your allies. When you use the weapon’s [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190) trait, you can point the clan dagger at an adjacent ally instead of defending yourself, creating a shield of runes around them. The runic barrier grants your ally the weapon’s circumstance bonus to AC, but you do not gain the bonus yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kin-Warding

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bloodbane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=539)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a  clan dagger 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bloodbane_ clan dagger is especially vicious against the ancestral enemies of the clan. When you damage an appropriate type of creature with the weapon, that creature takes 1 persistent bleed damage. The type of creature depends on the clan that made the dagger, but is typically drow, duergar, giant, or orc.

 In addition, whenever you inflict persistent bleed damage with a _bloodbane_ weapon (whether from its innate ability or in some other way), the weapon leaves ragged wounds. The bleeding creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become sickened 1. The creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bloodbane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=539)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a  clan dagger 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bloodbane_ clan dagger is especially vicious against the ancestral enemies of the clan. When you damage an appropriate type of creature with the weapon, that creature takes 1 persistent bleed damage. The type of creature depends on the clan that made the dagger, but is typically drow, duergar, giant, or orc.

 In addition, whenever you inflict persistent bleed damage with a _bloodbane_ weapon (whether from its innate ability or in some other way), the weapon leaves ragged wounds. The bleeding creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become sickened 1. The creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

---

This amulet is made from the scales of five ancient dragons. You gain resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and poison. If you take damage of one of these types from a dragon’s Breath Weapon, the amulet begins to glow brightly; for the next 10 minutes, your resistance against that type of damage increases to 20. If you are subjected to a dragon’s [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17), you can roll your saving throw twice and use the higher result to determine the aura’s effects. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You exude an aura that terrifies all foes in a 60-foot radius, as the [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17) ability with a DC 34 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonscale Amulet

>
[Guiding Chisel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=541)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=58" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 76

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical chisel evokes the holy symbol of the elven goddess of art and architecture, Findeladlara. The _Guiding Chisel’s_ original function was as a portal key to Duskgate, but as Alseta’s Ring faded from the collective memories of the elves, the chisel became known and used exclusively for its other abilities. Eventually, elven emissaries gave it to the dwarves of Saggorak as a gesture of friendship.

 You gain a +3 item bonus to Crafting checks related to stonemasonry. In addition, it takes you only 1 day to Craft items using the _Guiding Chisel_ instead of 4 days, and you quadruple the discount you get for spending additional days Crafting items with the _Guiding Chisel_. If it’s unclear whether you could use the _Guiding Chisel_ to Craft an item, the GM decides.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You embed the _Guiding Chisel_ in the ground and imagine a building. After 10 minutes, you remove the chisel and the building appears. The building can take any shape you wish, filling up to ten contiguous 10-foot cubes within 1,000 feet of the chisel. The building lasts until you use the chisel to create another building. This building has the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guiding Chisel

This [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) contains a smoldering core of magma that never cools. Whenever the _reforging shield’s_ current Hit Points are below its Break Threshold, the shield recovers 5 Hit Points at the start of its wielder’s turn each round until it is no longer [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2). Each time a character [Repairs](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) the shield, the shield recovers double the number of Hit Points. The shield has Hardness 15, HP 120, and BT 60.

This round shield is often fashioned to resemble the religious symbol of its dual-natured namesake. A _Nethysian bulwark_ is a [high-grade cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=311) [shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 10, HP 40, BT 20) that defends the wielder and can explode with retributive force when struck.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** The shield becomes broken when performing a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839);  **Effect** The shield explodes in a burst of bright light and shadowy tendrils, releasing a 15-foot cone of force that must include the attacker if possible. The cone deals 6d8 force damage to all creatures in the area (DC 34 basic Reflex save).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[False Death](/Equipment.aspx?ID=551)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Typically used to fake one’s death, this poison swiftly causes the imbiber to lose consciousness and suppresses vital signs. A creature examining the unconscious target must succeed at a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check against the imbiber’s [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC + 4 to determine that the target is alive, and a critical success allows the examiner to determine that a toxin is causing the effect. The false death toxin has an extremely bitter taste (Perception DC 10 to detect), making it difficult to trick a creature into consuming the poison against its wishes. 

**Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 5 days; **Stage 1** [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 hour); **Stage 3** unconscious (1 day)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: False Death

This oil, which is pressed from [vrock](/Monsters.aspx?ID=100) spores and mixed with a combination of acacia ash and thistle seeds, seeps into the skin of living creatures, initially inspiring irritability that unpredictably manifests as berserk rage.

[Aluum Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=560)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 82

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate pendant of brass is adorned with a vibrant blue gemstone. An _aluum charm_ grants control over a particular [aluum](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=113) and lesser influence over other such constructs. As long as you wear an aluum’s linked _aluum charm_, that aluum follows your verbal commands, including somewhat nuanced orders like “subdue this target” or “strike anyone wearing a blue robe.”

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** The charm grants you control over an [aluum](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=113) you can see within 60 feet, with a level equal to or lower than the charm. This has the effect of [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) and allows a DC 28 Will save. If the aluum is currently under the control of someone wearing its linked charm, it gets a result on its saving throw one degree higher than what it rolled. You can control only one aluum at a time using this activation, and controlling a new aluum ends the effect for one you had previously affected.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aluum Charm

    right="Item 3"

>
[Abadar's Flawless Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=562)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 121

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** follower of  Abadar 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This immaculate golden set of merchant’s scales is considered a divine instrument among Abadar’s most faithful. Unlike most scales, this one has only a single dish for placing small objects, and calculates value rather than weight. On the other side of the fulcrum, a steel arrow indicator points to various numbers engraved on the side of the scale. By placing an object of light Bulk onto the dish, you can determine its value in gold pieces in the market in which the scale was made (most of these scales are made in Katapesh or Absalom), based on its material composition and artisanship. For example, after placing a gemstone on the dish, the scale will indicate the fairest (average, in most cases) price you can expect to fetch in the market to which the scale is calibrated, but the scale wouldn’t detect the gemstone’s historical significance or any magical properties. _Abadar’s flawless scale_ can be calibrated to a different market by performing a 1-hour ritual in the proximity of that market.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Abadar's Flawless Scale

A magic item regarded as holy by followers of Cayden Cailean, this ornate metallic stein is always filled to the brim with delicious ale, no matter how much is drunk or spilled. If it is emptied (such as by being gulped quickly or upended onto the floor), the _bottomless stein_ will fill again within 1 round as long as it is right side up, though it’s impossible to transfer the ale to another container to sell it or store it for later. The exact type of ale with which the stein is filled is determined at the item’s creation, and cannot be changed thereafter.

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 121

**Price** 25 gp

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** follower of  Achaekek 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny serrated dagger looks like a miniature [sawtooth sabre](/Weapons.aspx?ID=65). Until activated, it is too small and ornamental to function as a weapon, which might allow it to be passed off as a weapon-shaped decoration.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You transform the _dagger of eternal sleep_ into a _+1 [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) striking dagger_ and Strike an unconscious target with the dagger. If you are not in an encounter or otherwise threatened and you roll a hit with your Strike, you get a critical hit instead. The damage from the Strike and the bleed from the critical specialization effect do not awaken the target, though the target becomes temporarily immune to this effect until the next time it wakes up, meaning further _daggers of eternal sleep_ awaken it as normal. The dagger disintegrates immediately after use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dagger of Eternal Sleep

    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deteriorating Dust](/Equipment.aspx?ID=565)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** follower of  Rovagug 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Contained in a specially enchanted small leather or hide sack, _deteriorating dust_ is a potent caustic agent and a prized item among Rovagug’s more discreet followers.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You sprinkle the _deteriorating dust_ over an unattended Medium or smaller object. The dust quickly fades to a transparent color and the object immediately begins to rust, melt, or otherwise fall apart. For the listed time, the object takes constant damage; if the object has a Hardness, then the dust gradually reduces the object’s Hardness instead. If the _deteriorating dust_ is still active after the object’s Hardness is reduced to 0, it deals damage to the object as previously described. If the object survives, reduced Hardness returns after the dust’s duration expires, but any damage remains.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dreadsmoke Thurible](/Equipment.aspx?ID=566)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 122

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** follower of  Urgathoa 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black-and-gray thurible is decorated with a skeleton motif; the jaw of one of the decorative skulls unhinges, allowing you to load it with incense as an action. A long, thin chain is connected to the top of the thurible.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact; **Cost** incense worth at least 5 gp;    **Effect** You swing the thurible around you, spreading inky black smoke in a 20-foot emanation that has the effect of _[obscuring mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210)_. Undead creatures can see through the smoke as if it didn’t exist. Negative energy also disrupts the magic of the smoke; anyone who uses a [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) effect, along with creatures affected by a negative effect, can see through the smoke for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dreadsmoke Thurible

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glaive of the Artist](/Equipment.aspx?ID=567)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 122

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

**Access** follower of  Shelyn 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) greater striking [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ has a long, multi-tailed rainbow-colored banner affixed to the butt of the pole in the style of [Shelyn’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) religious symbol. While wielding the glaive, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You furl one of the colored tails around the pole, causing the glaive to gain the effects of your choice of the [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_shocking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), or [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) property runes for 1 round, depending on the tail you chose.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You thrust the blade of the glaive into the ground, activating the power of the banner. (The blade can penetrate any non-metallic surface when used in this way, and does not leave a mark when withdrawn.) As long as the glaive is thrust into the ground and standing upright, all allies within 60 feet who can see the glaive’s banner gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glaive of the Artist

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 122

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn on wrists

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** follower of  Zon-Kuthon 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sturdy, [average manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) are connected by a thick iron chain that is much heavier than its appearance suggests at a glance. Followers of [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=20) sometimes employ _manacles of persuasion_ against captured enemies and spies. When the manacles are locked around an [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) creature’s wrists, they begin to sap the life out of the victim, dealing 2 negative damage per hour until the creature falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). The effect is [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), so the damage doesn’t cause the creature to become [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11). While the creature is unconscious, the manacles deal no damage and allow the creature to recover Hit Points normally to a maximum of 10 Hit Points, at which point the manacles begin to deal damage once more. The manacles have no effect on a creature that is not immobilized.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Manacles of Persuasion

---

This ornate headgear comes in many different shapes and sizes, though most are elaborately decorated with motifs of the deity whose honor in which it was made. The miter brings you spiritually closer to your deity’s servitors, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command);  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You chant for a minute about a task at hand for the coming week to cast a 4th-level [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) spell that grants you cryptic but useful advice.

</column>

This matching mortar and pestle are made from immaculate darkwood that looks nearly olive in the right light.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You use the _mortar of hidden meaning_ to grind tea leaves (or another suitable substance for making a hot beverage). While grinding the tea leaves, which takes no less than 1 minute, you can speak a message into the mortar. After the leaves are steeped in hot water, the first person who drinks from the resulting brew hears the message whispered in their ear.

[Necklace of Knives](/Equipment.aspx?ID=572)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Access** follower of  Pharasma 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This necklace strung with miniature stone throwing knives. As long as you wear it, you are never without a weapon, a crude surgical tool, or stake for a vampire, if necessary in following Pharasma’s teachings.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You pluck a miniature knife from the necklace, and it grows into a normal stone, steel, or wooden _[dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ for as long as you hold it, fading away 1 round after it leaves your hand. No matter how many knives you pull from the necklace, you never seem to deplete them. Wooden daggers from the necklace are as effective as ordinary steel daggers and can be useful to stake vampires.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Necklace of Knives

The haft of this _+2 striking [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)_ is carved with intricate designs of swarming rodents sacred to [Hanspur](/Deities.aspx?ID=154), the drowned god of the Sellen River. When used against a [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239), it ignores the swarm’s resistance to piercing damage, if any.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** While floating down a river on a raft or boat, you plunge the _rat-catcher trident_ into the water. The trident casts a 5th-level [_control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) spell on the body of water.

In true Iroran form, the simplicity of this small, inverted copper bell belies its flawlessness. When struck with a mallet (included with the singing bowl), it emits a harmonious tone that inspires bodily perfection in those who know how to hear it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16))    **Effect** While holding the _singing bowl of the versatile stance_ with one hand, you strike the lip of it with a mallet held in the other. >
[Torag's Silver Anvil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=575)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 123

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Meteor Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=97)
</row>

**Access** follower of  Torag 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This portable silver anvil has a narrow hoop on one end, through which a long, sturdy chain has been strung. _[Torag's](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) Silver Anvil_ can be wielded as a _+3 [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [meteor hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=97)_. Outside of combat, the anvil can be used with a hammer as an incredibly effective portable forge, heating up the metal to be forged without a furnace and granting a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks involving metalworking. When you use the anvil to successfully [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) a metal item, the item recovers an additional 10 Hit Points (or an additional 20 on a critical success).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Torag's Silver Anvil

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 73

**Price** 235 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Bo Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 striking [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17)_ is particularly long, making it useful as a balancing pole. While you wield it, the acrobat’s staff releases chalk dust to make your grip more secure, granting you a +1 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC against checks to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) you of it.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;   **Trigger** You fail or critically fail an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) and would fall;  **Effect** Reroll the Acrobatics check and take the better result.

When it’s activated, any allies within 10 feet who also just failed or critically failed a Reflex saving throw from the same source (such as a [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) spell) can also reroll their saving throw and use the better result.

A _bullhook_ is a stout rod about 4 feet long with a blunt hook on the end. Too dull to be useful as a weapon, a _bullhook_ is instead used to direct animals in training or in performing their tasks. A _bullhook_ grants you an item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) while you are holding it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wave the _bullhook_ to produce the effects of a [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell. This spell loses the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) trait and can target only animals.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _command_ of the appropriate level.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 73 **Price** 850 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This timeworn chalice is constructed of dull tin. It has an unassuming appearance save for the gold, silver, and ebony rings that encircle its rim. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The chalice fills with one of three different wines of your choosing, as described below. The wine looks, tastes, and smells the same regardless of which type you choose, and [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) has no effect on the liquid beyond indicating that it is magical.
  • Ordinary wine.
  • Wine that, when imbibed, casts a 2nd-level [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) spell on the drinker.
  • Wine that, when imbibed, casts a 4th-level [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell on the drinker.
If you aren’t chaotic, the first time each day you activate the chalice, there is a one-in-six chance that the liquid you conjure is a different type (the GM should roll 1d6 as a secret roll) instead. The second and third times you activate the chalice in the same day, the odds of this happening increase to two-in-six or three-in-six, respectively. If you are chaotic, the chalice always works as intended.
name: Grail of Twisted Desires

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Inspiring Spotlight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=585)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 73, [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 56

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _inspiring spotlight_ is a bulky contraption consisting of a drum-shaped metal housing around several reflective metal plates. Two handles on either side, each with a small button, allow you to aim the spotlight outward from your body.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** Once per hour;   **Effect** When you push the left-hand button, the _inspiring spotlight_ emits a beam of [bright magical light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 120-foot line that hangs in the air and remains in place even if you move the spotlight. If the line passes through an area of magical darkness or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the _inspiring spotlight_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness. Creatures within the line gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws and Charisma-based skill checks. In addition, creatures in the line who are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) reduce the value of their frightened condition by 1 at the beginning of each turn they start in the area of the line. The beam remains in place for 1 minute or until you push the right-hand button with another Interact action to extinguish the light.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Inspiring Spotlight

[Marvelous Calliope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=587)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 74

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large slide whistle appears to be made of fine brass, the sides of which are engraved with musical notes and dancing clowns.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, envision, Interact);     **Effect** You blow a tune upon the slide whistle, causing the whistle to expand. The whistle sprouts from one into several, and then creates a large wooden frame and keyboard. By the time the unfolding is complete, the whistle fully transforms into a calliope, a wagon-sized contraption consisting of several steam whistles played like an organ. The calliope is heavy and anchored in place.

 You have a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to play the calliope. The calliope has a tendency to produce music for a few seconds after you cease playing it, adding a jaunty and overlong conclusion to your music.

 If you use an Interact action to pull the small metal ring on the underside of the _marvelous calliope_, the calliope collapses back down into a slide whistle.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You play the calliope, causing it to cast [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) on one of the listeners.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Marvelous Calliope

This delicate and elegant diadem is woven of intricate silver wires set with three tear-shaped pearlescent moonstones. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> focus;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You are within 10 feet of a _moonstone pool_; **Effect** You peer into the _moonstone pool_ and gain access to all the visual history recorded within the receptacle by concentrating on the specific subject you wish to see. Conversely, you can choose to deposit your own memories into the pool by concentrating for 1 hour on the information you wish to impart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Moonstone Diadem

Ringmasters or announcers use _ringmaster’s staves_ to draw attention from their audiences or to salvage acts that fall flat mid-performance. While wielding a _ringmaster’s staff_, your normal voice can be clearly heard by all creatures within 300 feet regardless of intervening barriers or ambient noise, although your voice can’t penetrate magical silence and you can’t use this to extend an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) effect through barriers that would otherwise block it. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Saints' Balm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=590)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 75

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This luminescent paste is made from the powdered bones of a long-forgotten saint, mixed with herbs and blessed by a priest. It typically comes in a tightly sealed, palm-sized dish etched with holy symbols. Applying _saints’ balm_ to yourself or a creature within reach restores 3d8+10 Hit Points to the creature to which it’s applied.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Saints' Balm

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Spangled Rider's Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=591)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 75

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 resilient [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ is adorned with colorful sequins that sparkle in even the faintest light. While wearing the spangled rider’s suit, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) you are riding. Any animal you ride automatically knows the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=78) and [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=87) basic actions, in addition to any other basic actions it knows (normally only Leap, Seek, Stand, Stride, and Strike).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114))  **Trigger** You take damage while mounted or your mount Drops Prone;  **Effect** You dismount, moving off your mount into a space adjacent to it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spangled Rider's Suit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Stole of Civility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=592)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 75

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Woven from swaths of rich brocade silk and embroidered with ancient Azlanti script in golden thread, this stole imparts a noble appearance to even the homeliest of individuals when draped over the shoulders. While wearing a stole of civility, you receive a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) with humans or [Make a Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of humans, and a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against humans. The stole also grants you full understanding of the [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49) language.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** you are a human; **Effect** You gain 10 temporary Hit Points, which last for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stole of Civility

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Whip of Compliance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=593)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 75

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held, 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The handle of this braided leather _+1 striking [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)_ is made from the hairs of a variety of rare and mythical animals. When used against an animal, the _whip of compliance_ can potentially force the target to follow your commands.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** Until the end of your current turn, any animals you Strike with the whip must attempt a DC 25 Will save. On a failed or critically failed saving throw, the whip casts a 4th-level [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) spell on the animal with a base duration of 1 round, and the spell loses the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) trait. The course of action must still fulfill _suggestion’s_ requirements. If you can cast spells, you can expend one of your spells of 4th level or higher as a part of activating the whip; if you do so, the _suggestions_ this turn have their normal base duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whip of Compliance

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A dwarven daisy is a string of dozens of firecrackers that create tiny, loud explosions. The string ends with a short, wrapped fuse. Pulling off the wrapping in a quick motion lights the fuse and is done as part of the action to Strike. A dwarven daisy deals the listed fire damage and splash damage. A dwarven daisy also has a chance to [dazzle](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) its target. A creature struck by a dwarven daisy must succeed at a Fortitude save or become dazzled for 1 round.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dwarven Daisy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=594)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Quest #5: The Dragon Who Stole Evoking Day](/Sources.aspx?ID=27) pg. 14

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A dwarven daisy is a string of dozens of firecrackers that create tiny, loud explosions. The string ends with a short, wrapped fuse. Pulling off the wrapping in a quick motion lights the fuse and is done as part of the action to Strike. A dwarven daisy deals the listed fire damage and splash damage. A dwarven daisy also has a chance to [dazzle](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) its target. A creature struck by a dwarven daisy must succeed at a Fortitude save or become dazzled for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Quest #5: The Dragon Who Stole Evoking Day](/Sources.aspx?ID=27) pg. 14

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The firework deals 1d6 fire damage and 1 fire splash damage. The DC is 16.

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The firework deals 2d6 fire damage and 2 fire splash damage. The DC is 18.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Genius Diadem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=595)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 89

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn circlet

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +22; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet, constant [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), and eight other common languages)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +34, seven [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skills +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +28

**Int** +8, **Wis** +0, **Cha** +4

**Will** +24 

</column>

---        

The _genius diadem_ is a [_diadem of intellect_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=401) that typically acts like an arrogant professor or mentor, often boasting that it is a certified greater intellect and far superior to your own intelligence, even after the benefits the diadem grants you. The _genius diadem_ encourages you to learn things for yourself rather than actually attempting to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) for you, though you or your allies might be able to play on its arrogance using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to trick it into doing so. In addition to allowing you to activate it, the diadem can use the _diadem of intellect’s_ [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) activation with its own actions, though if it does so, the diadem gains the benefits instead of you. Only the diadem can use the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The _genius diadem_ casts 7th-level [_feeblemind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=112).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Genius Diadem

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs=""
>
[Martyr's Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=596)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="LG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 89

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +12; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13

**Int** +0, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +4

**Will** +15 

</column>

---        

A _martyr’s shield_ is a [_lesser sturdy shield_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=327) imbued with the compassion of a devout champion of a righteous deity, like [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9) or [Vildeis](/Deities.aspx?ID=130), who sacrificed themself to save an ally. In addition to good-naturedly attempting to convert you to its religion, the _martyr’s shield_ can use 1 reaction each round that, when raised, it uses to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) to protect an ally adjacent to you, with the effects of the [Shield Warden](/Feats.aspx?ID=229) feat. The _martyr’s shield_ uses this reaction whether you would prefer it did so or not. The _martyr’s shield_ can be upgraded to a stronger form of _sturdy shield_ by paying the difference in cost between its current type of _sturdy shield_ and the new type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Martyr's Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs=""
>
[Singing Sword](/Equipment.aspx?ID=597)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 89

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +11; precise vision 30 feet, imprecise hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and four other common languages)

**Skills** [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15

**Int** +2, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +4

**Will** +13 (+17 against attempts to quiet its singing)

</column>

---        

A _singing sword_ is a _+1 striking [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_ imbued with the consciousness of a boisterous bard, and therefore constantly sings at all times. A _singing sword_ can’t stop singing and in fact communicates in no way other than by singing. A successful [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against its Will DC can convince it to quiet its singing to a whisper for 10 minutes, or 20 minutes on a critical success, though it quickly grows displeased at anyone who attempts this repeatedly. The _singing sword_ can spend its actions to attack on its own, with the effects of a [_dancing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=293) weapon’s activation, except that its attack modifier is +12. Additionally, it can perform the following activations; each casts a composition spell and follows all the usual rules for [compositions](/Traits.aspx?ID=31).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The _singing sword_ casts [_inspire courage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=386).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The _singing sword_ casts [_inspire defense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=387).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You or an ally within 60 feet rolls a saving throw against an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) effect;  **Effect** The _singing sword_ casts [_counter performance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=381).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Singing Sword

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs=""
>
[Bag of Devouring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=598)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 90

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item appears to be and functions as a [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249), but is actually a feeding orifice of a bizarre extradimensional creature. Any animal or vegetable matter put in the bag has a chance of triggering the bag's interest. Whenever you reach into the bag to retrieve an item or place an animal (or plant or animal or plant product) within the bag, roll a DC 9 flat check. On a success, the bag ignores the intrusion. On a failure, the bag devours the triggering material, removing it from this realm of existence; the bag can't eat [artifacts](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45) or other similarly hard-to-destroy items. If the triggering material is not entirely inside of the bag, such as when someone reaches a hand inside, the _bag of devouring_ attempts to pull it completely inside the bag using a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) action, with an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) bonus determined by the type of bag. On a success, it devours the victim or object entirely.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs=""
>
Bag of Devouring Type I
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 90

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

**Athletics Bonus** +15; acts as a _type II bag of holding_

<title
level="2"

pfs=""
>
Bag of Devouring Type II
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

**Athletics Bonus** +21; acts as a _type III bag of holding_

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs=""
>
Bag of Devouring Type III
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 90

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

**Athletics Bonus** +24; acts as a _type IV bag of holding_
name: Bag of Devouring

---

This item appears to be and functions as a [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249), but is actually a feeding orifice of a bizarre extradimensional creature. Any animal or vegetable matter put in the bag has a chance of triggering the bag's interest. Whenever you reach into the bag to retrieve an item or place an animal (or plant or animal or plant product) within the bag, roll a DC 9 flat check. On a success, the bag ignores the intrusion. On a failure, the bag devours the triggering material, removing it from this realm of existence; the bag can't eat [artifacts](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45) or other similarly hard-to-destroy items. If the triggering material is not entirely inside of the bag, such as when someone reaches a hand inside, the _bag of devouring_ attempts to pull it completely inside the bag using a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) action, with an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) bonus determined by the type of bag. On a success, it devours the victim or object entirely.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Bag of Weasels](/Equipment.aspx?ID=599)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 90

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item appears to be and functions as a [_type I bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249), until you try to retrieve an item from the bag. Whenever you retrieve an item from the _bag of weasels_, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, you retrieve the item as normal. On a failure, the item you retrieve is transformed into a weasel; this doesn’t affect artifacts, cursed items, or other hard-to-destroy items.

 This weasel has the statistics of a [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=346), except that it is Tiny and exudes an aura of transmutation magic. The weasel has no loyalty to you and typically attempts to escape as quickly as possible. If the weasel is targeted with a [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) spell or a similar magical effect, it transforms back into the original item that was taken from the _bag of weasels_. If it dies or is slain, the weasel disappears and the item is permanently destroyed. Because the weasel is a transformed item, you don’t gain any benefit you would receive from attacking a creature, defeating one, damaging one, or the like, but you do gain any benefit you would gain from destroying an item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bag of Weasels

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

</row>

---

These boots act as [_greater boots of elvenkind_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=413), but they react wildly to strong physical exertion. While you wear the boots, the curse activates whenever you attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check or Stride more than once in a single round during an encounter. The boots cast an 8th-level [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) spell on you, and you automatically fail your save. Once the curse has activated for the first time, the boots fuse to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs=""
>
[Cloak of Immolation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=601)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Appearing as a magic cloak such as a [_cloak of elvenkind_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=424), this garment is made of highly volatile fabric. While wearing it, if you take fire damage, you also take 1d10 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). Taking fire damage while the persistent fire damage is in effect has no additional effect. You can extinguish the persistent fire damage as normal.

 Any creature that hits you with a melee unarmed attack while you are taking this persistent fire damage takes fire damage equal to the persistent fire damage you took on your previous turn. Once the curse has activated for the first time, the boots fuse to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloak of Immolation

[Gloves of Carelessness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=602)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These gloves appear and function as [_gloves of storing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=437) but repel any item not stored in the gloves. When wearing them, whenever you Interact to draw or pick up an item, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, you do so as normal. On a failure, you instead fling the item 15 feet in a random direction. You can use a reaction to attempt a DC 23 Reflex save, causing the item to land at your feet instead if you succeed. Removing the stored item from the gloves does not trigger the item’s curse. The first time the curse activates, the gloves fuse to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gloves of Carelessness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Monkey's Paw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=604)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dried, gnarled hand is clenched in a fist, waiting for a creature to pick it up. When you pick up the _monkey’s paw_, the hand opens, revealing three withered fingers. The _monkey’s paw_ grants you three wishes, curling one finger after every one. Once you pick up the _monkey’s paw_, you cannot discard the hand until it returns to a clenched fist by casting its three wishes. Any attempts to discard the hand, even with the effects of a [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell, are unsuccessful as the _monkey’s paw_ reappears among your possessions within 1d4 hours; it doesn’t work for any other creature in the intervening time. The hand returns even if another creature steals it from you. Once you make all three wishes, the _monkey’s paw_ uses [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) to travel to a random point in the multiverse.

 Whenever the _monkey’s paw_ hears you utter a statement that sounds like a wish, even if you don’t use the words “I wish,” it activates and grants a twisted, horrifying version of your wish, producing any effect within the possibility of [_alter reality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=8), _miracle_, [_primal phenomenon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=231), or _wish_, and potentially a greater effect at the GM’s discretion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Monkey's Paw

>
[Necklace of Strangulation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=605)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn necklace

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This beautiful necklace appears to be a magical item such as a [_necklace of fireballs_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=449) or a [_pendant of the occult_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=450). When invested, it magically locks in place, suspiciously tightly, and fuses to you. When you enter a situation of extreme stress (as determined by the GM), the necklace tightens around your neck, [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) you and dealing 30 bludgeoning damage to you at the end of each of your turns. Once per round, you can spend a single action to attempt a DC 34 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check or Fortitude save; success means you don’t take the damage on your current turn, but you continue suffocating. The necklace loosens after you’ve been dead for a month.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Necklace of Strangulation

---

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs=""
>
[Ring of Truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=607)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ring appears to be a [_ring of lies_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=456), but when invested, close investigation reveals angelic wings and divine creatures hidden on the face of the ring. When you invest the ring, you are rendered unable to tell a deliberate lie, in either speech or writing. If you attempt to omit the truth or phrase things truthfully but deceptively, roll a DC 11 flat check; on a failure, the ring itself blurts out the entire truth (as you believe it) as an answer. Keeping silent does not activate the ring’s curse. Once the curse has activated for the first time, the ring fuses to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Truth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs=""
>
[Stone of Weight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=608)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 92

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Also known as a loadstone, this small stone appears nonmagical and has a lovely sheen, giving the impression that it could be a valuable or a notable item or a magic stone of some kind. When you’ve carried the stone on your person for 1 minute, its curse activates: the stone’s size does not change, but it suddenly increases in weight to 5 Bulk. This additional weight does not cause the stone to deal more damage if thrown or used as a weapon. It reappears in your possessions within 1 minute if you discard it, and can’t be destroyed or thrown away permanently, or even placed in a container that would reduce or negate its Bulk (like a [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249)), until it is subject to a [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) spell or similar magic effect. Once the curse has activated for the first time, the stone fuses to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stone of Weight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 26"
    pfs=""
>
[Axe of the Dwarven Lords](/Equipment.aspx?ID=609)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 106

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dwarven War Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=62)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+4 major striking [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [dwarven waraxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=62)_ is carved with an intricate design depicting countless generations of great dwarven warriors and leaders. The axe has the [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon trait, in addition to the normal weapon traits for a dwarven waraxe. Strikes with the axe deal an additional 1d6 damage to orcs. While the axe is in your possession, you gain a +4 item bonus when [Crafting](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) armor, jewelry, metalworking, stonemasonry, snares, traps, and weapons. If you are a [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1), you gain [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) while holding the axe. If you are not a dwarf, you are [stupefied 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) while holding the axe, and if you are an orc, you are also [drained 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while holding it.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** The axe casts a 10th-level [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) spell to conjure an [elite elemental avalanche](/Monsters.aspx?ID=186). The spell is automatically sustained, requiring no action on your part but still allowing you to command the elemental on each of your turns. You can Dismiss the Spell.

**Destruction** If an orc beheads a dwarven king with the _Axe of the Dwarven Lords_, the axe rusts away to nothing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Axe of the Dwarven Lords

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>
</additional-info>

---

The blade of this  +4 major striking  keen   returning   speed   dwarven waraxe   is carved with an intricate design depicting countless generations …
